name: Coleman Slide
history: A hands-down slide on a longboard used to control speed going downhill. Named after Cliff Coleman who developed it for downhill safety.

name: Toeside Slide
history: A longboard slide where the toe edge of the board is used to initiate a sideways sliding stop. A fundamental downhill control technique.

name: Heelside Slide
history: A longboard slide initiated from the heel edge. The counterpart to the toeside slide, used for speed control on descents.

name: Stand-up Slide
history: A longboard slide performed without putting hands down on the pavement. Considered more advanced than the hands-down Coleman slide.
youtube_query: stand up slide longboard downhill tutorial

name: Pendulum Slide
history: A longboard slide where the board swings 180 degrees and swings back to continue in the original direction, resembling a pendulum.

name: Cheeky Slide
history: A switch heelside slide on a longboard. Performed with the non-dominant foot forward, adding difficulty to the slide.

name: Bluntslide (Longboard)
history: A slide on a longboard where the tail of the board drags during a slide. Adapted from street skating terminology to describe specific longboard slide positions.

name: Ghost Ride
history: The skater steps off the moving longboard, lets it roll briefly, then steps back on. A stylish longboard trick showing board control and confidence.

name: Cross-Step
history: Walking across the longboard by crossing one foot over the other while rolling. A fundamental longboard dancing move.

name: Peter Pan
history: A longboard dancing trick where one foot steps to the nose while the other leg swings out like a flying pose. Named for its resemblance to the fictional character's flying pose.
youtube_query: peter pan longboard dancing trick tutorial

name: Pirouette
history: A 360-degree spin performed while standing on a longboard, similar to a ballet pirouette. A longboard dancing move.

name: Cannonball
history: Crouching into the tightest possible tuck position while bombing a hill to maximize speed. The fundamental aerodynamic position for downhill speed.

name: Longboard Kickflip
history: A kickflip on a longboard. Much harder than on a street deck due to the board's length and weight.

name: No Comply 180 (Longboard)
history: A no comply with a 180-degree rotation on a longboard. Adapted from street skating to show creativity on longboards.

name: Shove-it (Longboard)
history: A shove-it on a longboard. The board rotates 180 degrees while the skater hops and lands back on it. More difficult on a long board.

name: Speedcheck
history: A short, controlled slide used to bleed off speed during a downhill run without stopping completely. An essential downhill safety technique.

name: Sitdown Slide
history: A slide performed from a sitting or very low crouching position on a longboard. Used to stay low and control speed safely.

name: Switch Coleman
history: A Coleman slide performed in switch stance. Adds difficulty to the standard hands-down slide.

name: Boardwalk Slide
history: A slide unique to longboarding where the board goes through a series of controlled sideways motions down a hill. Used for stylistic control.

name: Kickback
history: A footbrake technique where the dragging foot kicks backward to slow down. An alternative to slides for speed management.

name: Nose Press
history: Pressing the nose of the board down onto an obstacle while sliding. A variation between a noseslide and a nosegrind.

name: Tailpressure Flip
history: The tail is pressed down with the back foot and used to generate a flip rather than a traditional ollie pop. Creates a unique board rotation.

name: Frontside 360 Shove-it
history: The board rotates 360 degrees frontside without a body rotation. A rarely-seen board-only spin trick.

name: Nollie 360 Shove-it
history: A 360-degree shove-it from nollie stance. The nose is popped and the board completes a full backside rotation.

name: Fakie 360 Shove-it
history: A 360-degree shove-it from fakie stance. The board spins while the skater rolls backward.

name: Switch 360 Shove-it
history: A 360 shove-it in switch stance. The board rotates fully with the non-dominant foot leading.

name: Tailwhip
history: The tail of the board is kicked sideways in a wide arc (whipping motion) rather than a standard shove-it. Primarily used in freestyle and scooter-influenced skating.

name: Primo to Kickflip
history: The board is balanced on its rail in primo position, then kickflipped back to normal. A Rodney Mullen-influenced freestyle combination.

name: Nollie Hardflip Backside
history: A nollie hardflip with a backside orientation. One of the most obscure nollie hardflip variations.

name: Switch Inward Heelflip
history: An inward heelflip performed in switch stance. Among the rarest switch flip tricks in skateboarding.

name: Fakie Inward Heelflip
history: An inward heelflip from fakie stance. The backside shove-it and heelflip are both executed while rolling backward.

name: Nollie Hardflip Frontside
history: A nollie frontside hardflip. An extremely rare and technical nollie variation.

name: Oververt
history: Going past vertical on a vert ramp, meaning the skater is briefly upside down relative to the ramp. Requires a very steep vert ramp and advanced skill.

name: Late Kickflip
history: A kickflip where the flick happens after the peak of the ollie, causing a 'late' snap of the board. Creates a unique visual timing.

name: Late Heelflip
history: A heelflip flicked after the peak of the ollie. The delayed rotation gives the trick a stylish late snap appearance.

name: Late Shove-it
history: A shove-it executed after the peak of the ollie. The late timing gives a distinctive look to this simple trick.
youtube_query: late shove it skateboard trick tutorial

name: Late 180
history: A 180-degree body rotation initiated after the peak of the ollie. Adds a stylistic element of timing to the standard 180.

name: Tailblock
history: The tail of the board blocks against the coping while the board pauses at the top of the ramp. An old-school vert and pool lip trick.

name: Nosepick (Vert)
history: The nose of the board presses against the coping while the tail hangs in the air. A stylish nose-first lip trick on vert ramps and pools.

name: Sweeper
history: A trick where the board is swept out to the side while airborne, then swept back under the feet. An old-school vert trick.

name: Lipslide (Vert)
history: A lipslide on the coping of a vert ramp. The board goes over the coping and slides along it perpendicular to the ramp.

name: Losi Grind
history: A backside 5-0 where the front foot is placed on the nose while grinding, named after Eric Loskop. A technical hybrid between a 5-0 and a noseblunt.

name: Suski Grind
history: A frontside 5-0 grind where the nose is also pressing against the obstacle. Named after Eric Dressen by some accounts. A grind between a 5-0 and a nosegrind.

name: Switch Hurricane
history: A hurricane grind in switch stance. An extremely advanced grind combining stance difficulty with the complexity of the hurricane.

name: Cab Nosegrind
history: A fakie 360 (Caballerial) into a nosegrind. Combining the spin with the grind makes for a very creative trick.

name: Bigspin Boardslide
history: A bigspin (backside 360 body + backside 360 board) into a boardslide. An extremely technical and rare street trick.

name: Frontside Bigspin Noseslide
history: A frontside bigspin into a noseslide. Combining the full spin with a precise nose-lock makes this exceptionally difficult.

name: Ollie to Grind
history: Any ollie that enters directly into a grind. The clean ollie-to-grind entry is fundamental to street and ledge skating.
youtube_query: ollie to grind skateboard ledge tutorial

name: Switch 5-0 Grind
history: A 5-0 grind in switch stance. The weaker foot is at the back while only the back truck grinds.

name: Nollie 50-50
history: A 50-50 grind entered from nollie stance. The nose pop launches the skater into the double-truck grind position.

name: Fakie 50-50
history: A 50-50 grind from fakie stance. Rolling backward into a grind requires strong spatial awareness.

name: Primo Flip to Regular
history: A primo position that is flipped back to regular board orientation. Popularized by freestyle specialists in the 1980s.
youtube_query: primo flip to regular skateboard freestyle

name: Butterflip
history: A trick where the board flips in a butterfly-like pattern. A very obscure and rarely categorized old-school freestyle trick.

name: Spacewalk to Casper
history: A spacewalk (alternating side-to-side pumping) that transitions into a Casper stall position. A flowing freestyle combination.

name: Fingerflip to Casper
history: A fingerflip that lands in Casper (board upside-down). Combines the hand-assisted flip with the inverted board balance.
